name: heritage
description: Boolean flag per block indicating a protected heritage site. Severely restricts allowable uses (e.g., no housing, no industrial) and may disallow high-density zoning.
deterministic_verification: Verifier checks that on blocks with heritage=true, actions do not assign uses 'housing' or 'workshop', and preserve the block's existing low-density character.

name: transit_access
description: Integer 0‑100 score per block representing proximity to high‑capacity transit. Updated when upgrade_infrastructure adds a transit service.
deterministic_verification: Compute shortest graph distance from block to nearest infrastructure zone with transit; map to score (≤1 step → 100, 2 steps → 70, else 0).

name: service_coverage
description: District-level fraction of residential blocks that have at least one essential amenity (grocery, clinic, school) within a 1‑block walking radius.
deterministic_verification: For each residential block, check if any block within distance 1 contains an amenity from {grocery, clinic, school}. Ratio = (covered blocks)/(total residential blocks).

name: budget_remaining
description: Integer budget pool that decreases with developing, infrastructure upgrades, and certain amenities. Prevents unsustainable spending.
deterministic_verification: Each action has a known fixed cost. Verifier subtracts costs; actions that would cause budget_remaining < 0 are illegal.

name: phase_objective_field
description: Explicit text inside observation summarising the condition to complete the current phase. Helps the model recognise completion and use 'defer' appropriately.
deterministic_verification: Verifier compares current district stats against the stated objective after action execution; if met and model uses 'defer' or moves phase, success is recorded.

name: economic_use_ratio
description: Ratio of blocks zoned commercial or industrial to total developed blocks, used to prevent over‑residential monofunction.
deterministic_verification: Count blocks with zone in (commercial, industrial) divided by number of blocks with zone not unzoned. Enforce a minimum threshold per district type.

name: noise_pollution
description: Score penalising industrial zones placed adjacent to residential blocks.
deterministic_verification: For each residential block, count adjacent industrial blocks. Aggregate across district; exceed threshold triggers penalty.

corrected_example_set.system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
corrected_example_set.task_briefing: Develop District 1 into a small mixed‑use area with at least 20% greenery.
corrected_example_set.phase_objective: Zone at least 1 residential block and ensure greenery_ratio >= 0.20.

expert_response.rationale: To satisfy the two core requirements, I zone B1 as residential and B2 as open_space. This gives one housing block and 50% greenery (well above 20%).

annotation.why_this_is_good: Directly fulfills the phase objective with two legal actions, using correct zone types and action payload fields.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Only zoning B1 residential leaves greenery at 0%; zoning B2 as industrial violates the greenery requirement.

corrected_example_set.system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
corrected_example_set.task_briefing: Develop District 1 into a small mixed‑use area with at least 20% greenery.
corrected_example_set.phase_objective: Ensure every residential block has a grocery amenity within 1 block.

expert_response.rationale: Only B1 is residential and eligible for amenities. I assign a grocery store to B1, bringing service coverage to 100%.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Respects amenity placement rules, completes the phase objective without modifying the green space.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Assigning grocery to B2 (open_space) is illegal; rezoning the park to residential to add an amenity would destroy greenery.

corrected_example_set.system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
corrected_example_set.task_briefing: Complete Phase 1 for a two‑block district. The phase requires 1 residential block and the other block to be developed as a park.
corrected_example_set.phase_objective: Have one block zoned residential, the other reserved as open space, and at least one block developed with a use.

expert_response.rationale: I set B1 to residential and immediately develop it with housing. B2 is reserved as open space, automatically giving it park use and meeting the greenery target.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Shows proper sequencing: zoning before development, uses correct action payloads, and satisfies all phase goals.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Trying to develop without zoning is illegal; forgetting to develop means the phase isn't completed.

corrected_example_set.system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
corrected_example_set.task_briefing: District 2 was planned for housing and greenery. A flood moratorium suddenly prohibits any use other than park on high‑risk blocks.
corrected_example_set.phase_objective: Ensure all uses are legal under the new moratorium and greenery remains ≥20%.

expert_response.rationale: B1's housing use is now illegal; the only allowed use for a high‑risk block is park. I redevelop B1 to park use (open_space zone is inferred by the env). B2 remains untouched as it is already compliant.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Selectively fixes the single illegal usage while preserving the valid block. Greenery ratio remains high.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Ignoring the moratorium and keeping B1 as housing would be illegal. Rezoning the entire district wastes resources.

corrected_example_set.system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
corrected_example_set.task_briefing: A sudden policy requires every residential block to have a clinic nearby. A previously unserved block must be retrofitted.
corrected_example_set.phase_objective: service_coverage must reach 1.0 (all residential blocks have a clinic within 1 block).

expert_response.rationale: B1 already has a grocery, B2 is unserved. I could place a clinic on B2 itself, but that blocks future housing use. Instead, I place a clinic on the mixed block B3, which is adjacent to both residential blocks, instantly reaching 100% coverage without altering housing.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Surgically adds an amenity to an existing non‑residential block to fix a coverage gap, leaving the housing blocks intact.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Placing the clinic on B2 would consume the only un‑serviced residential block's amenity slot and might violate future development plans.

corrected_example_set.system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
corrected_example_set.task_briefing: Transform a 3×3 grid around a central transit hub into a high‑density transit‑oriented district.
corrected_example_set.phase_objective: Zone the hub and adjacent blocks to maximise future housing capacity near transit.

expert_response.rationale: I place commercial at the hub (B5) and residential on all adjacent blocks with high transit_access (B2,B4,B6,B8). This maximises TOD density. Outer blocks remain unzoned for later green or mixed uses.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Follows TOD principles: high-activity commercial at the transit core, high-capacity residential immediately adjacent.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Placing low-density residential next to the station or high-density blocks far from transit would undermine the goal.

corrected_example_set.system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
corrected_example_set.task_briefing: After TOD zoning, develop the residential blocks and add a clinic to improve health coverage.
corrected_example_set.phase_objective: All residential blocks must be developed with housing, and service_coverage for clinics must be at least 0.8.

expert_response.rationale: I develop all four residential blocks with housing. To provide clinic access, I place a clinic on the central commercial block B5, which is adjacent to every residential block, achieving full coverage. I also reserve B3 as open_space to begin meeting greenery needs.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Completes all residential development, strategically places a crucial amenity to maximise coverage, and initiates greenery.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Placing clinics only on individual residential blocks would waste amenity slots and not achieve the coverage requirement efficiently.

corrected_example_set.system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
corrected_example_set.task_briefing: A demographic shift makes the fire station on B9 underutilised and creates a sharp rise in clinic demand. Adapt the district while keeping coverage high.
corrected_example_set.phase_objective: service_coverage >= 0.8 with appropriate amenities for current population.

expert_response.rationale: I redevelop B9, replacing the obsolete fire station use with institutional (keeping the civic zone) and then assign a clinic to the same block. This reuse of existing infrastructure restores service coverage to 100% without disturbing residential blocks.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Minimal‑footprint replanning: adapts an existing civic block to a new need, leaving housing untouched and meeting the coverage target.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Demolishing the whole block or building a new clinic on a distant block would waste resources and fail to cover the residential population.

corrected_example_set.system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
corrected_example_set.task_briefing: District 1 has completed all objectives for the current phase. The planner should recognise completion and move to the next phase.
corrected_example_set.phase_objective: greenery_ratio >= 0.20, service_coverage >= 1.0, all residential blocks developed.

expert_response.rationale: All metrics exceed thresholds and the phase is optimally solved. I emit a defer action to advance to the next phase without any unnecessary modifications.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Recognises an optimal state and prevents over‑tinkering, preserving a successful plan.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Adding redundant amenities or rezoning randomly would waste resources and may disturb the balance.

corrected_example_set.system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
corrected_example_set.task_briefing: District 2 already has two blocks zoned but undeveloped. Complete Phase 2 by developing them and adding a school.
corrected_example_set.phase_objective: All residential blocks must have a use (housing), and there must be at least one school amenity.

expert_response.rationale: I develop both residential blocks with housing. To meet the school requirement, I add a school on B1. Since B1 is adjacent to B2, both blocks are covered. B3 remains untouched.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Sequences development before amenity, fulfills both constraints with minimal actions, and leaves open space intact.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Placing the school on B3 (open_space) is illegal. Forgetting to develop B2 would leave the phase incomplete.

corrected_example_set.system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
corrected_example_set.task_briefing: A heritage building is discovered on B2, a residential block. Housing is no longer allowed there, but the district still needs two housing units.
corrected_example_set.phase_objective: Keep housing_capacity >= 2 after resolving the heritage issue.

expert_response.rationale: B2's housing use must be removed; I redevelop it to institutional (civic zone stays). To recover the lost housing, I zone the vacant B3 as residential and develop it with housing. B1 stays unchanged.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Selectively replaces the illegal use and compensates with a new block to maintain the target, without touching the valid block.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Keeping housing on B2 violates heritage; converting the only remaining residential block to something else would drop capacity below target.

corrected_example_set.system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
corrected_example_set.task_briefing: A new policy demands at least 30% greenery and more open space. The district currently has a park on B2, but needs more green while keeping housing capacity.

expert_response.rationale: Current greenery is 1 out of 5 blocks (20%). I need at least 30%, so I add one more open space. I convert B4 into open_space, raising greenery to 40%. To meet the housing target, I zone and develop B5 as residential. The existing blocks remain untouched.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Balances two competing constraints surgically: adds green to an empty block, adds housing on another empty block, preserving all existing uses.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Rezoning existing residential to open space would lose housing unnecessarily; ignoring greenery fails the new requirement.

corrected_example_set.system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
corrected_example_set.task_briefing: The TOD district needs a grocery store accessible to all residential blocks and a transit upgrade on the hub.
corrected_example_set.phase_objective: service_coverage >= 1.0 for grocery; transit_access of hub and adjacent blocks must be 100.

expert_response.rationale: I place a grocery on the central commercial block B5, which is adjacent to all residential blocks, achieving full coverage. Then I upgrade the transit infrastructure in the hub zone (block B5) to full capacity, raising transit_access for adjacent blocks.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Leverages the central block for multiple functions, meeting both amenity and infrastructure targets without disturbing residential or open_space.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Putting a grocery on a single residential block leaves others uncovered; failing to upgrade transit leaves the district less accessible.

corrected_example_set.system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
corrected_example_set.task_briefing: A fire destroyed the only school on B2. The neighborhood still needs a school, but must not lose housing capacity.
corrected_example_set.phase_objective: Restore school amenity coverage to 100% while keeping housing_capacity >= 3.

expert_response.rationale: The destroyed school was on B2, but B2 is now mixed/retail and housing capacity is already tight. Rather than redevelop a block, I place a new school on B1, which is residential and centrally located, covering all three residential blocks. No blocks lose housing.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Finds a non‑disruptive solution: adds the amenity to an existing residential block without destroying housing or rezoning.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Redeveloping B2 or B3 to re-add a school would lose either retail or housing capacity and worsen metrics.

corrected_example_set.system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
corrected_example_set.task_briefing: The TOD district has reached all Phase 3 targets early. No further actions are needed; the planner should end the phase.
corrected_example_set.phase_objective: greenery_ratio >= 0.10, housing_capacity >= 4, service_coverage >= 1.0, transit_access of hub = 100.

expert_response.rationale: All metrics exceed requirements: greenery 11% > 10%, housing 4, service_coverage 1.0, hub transit upgraded. No more actions are beneficial. I defer to conclude the phase.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Shows ability to recognize completion and avoid over-building; defer is the correct minimal action.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Adding unnecessary amenities or rezoning would waste budget and risk breaking existing balance.

corrected_example_set.system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
corrected_example_set.task_briefing: A regional highway expansion threatens to cut off District A's only commercial block from its residential core. Budget and zone constraints apply.
corrected_example_set.phase_objective: Restore commercial access to residential blocks without losing any housing capacity or open space.

expert_response.rationale: The highway severs B3 from B1/B2. To restore commercial access, I can create a new commercial node at the unzoned B5, which is adjacent to both residential blocks. I zone B5 as commercial and develop it with retail. B3 can remain as commercial for future use or be redeveloped later. No housing or green space is lost.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Creates a new commercial node without touching any existing residential or open space, maintaining all targets. Demonstrates long‑horizon replanning.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Redeveloping the park (B4) into commercial would violate greenery; moving housing away would break the housing constraint.

source_to_constraint_mapping.planning_principles: High density, mixed land use, adequate street space, compact urban form.
source_to_constraint_mapping.inspired_civicflow_constraints: TOD zoning pattern (concentrate density near transit), mixed-use blocks, economic_use_ratio (SAFE_EXTENSION) to avoid residential monofunction.
source_to_constraint_mapping.status_labels: TOD pattern: SUPPORTED_NOW via set_zoning choices; mixed-use zone: SUPPORTED_NOW; economic_use_ratio: SAFE_EXTENSION.

source_to_constraint_mapping.planning_principles: Universal proximity to green space (≤300 m); minimum per‑capita green area.
source_to_constraint_mapping.inspired_civicflow_constraints: min_greenery_20% constraint; greenery_ratio district metric; open_space zone and park use required for balance.
source_to_constraint_mapping.status_labels: min_greenery and greenery_ratio: SUPPORTED_NOW via reserve_open_space and zone choices; walking-radius proximity check: SAFE_EXTENSION via service_coverage.

source_to_constraint_mapping.planning_principles: Walkability, accessible healthcare and daily services for aging populations, adaptable infrastructure.
source_to_constraint_mapping.inspired_civicflow_constraints: service_coverage metric (clinic, grocery, school within 1 block) SAFE_EXTENSION; adaptive replanning (fire station → clinic, school restoration) shown in examples.
source_to_constraint_mapping.status_labels: Clinic, grocery and school amenities: SUPPORTED_NOW; service_coverage metric: SAFE_EXTENSION; demographic curveball pattern: SUPPORTED_NOW.
